_comment: Policy rules for Australian real estate investment. Evaluated during AI committee playbook generation.

rules.description: High-rise developments restricted in heritage-listed areas
rules.message: Heritage overlay applies — high-rise development may be restricted. Verify with local council before relying on capital growth projections.

rules.description: Flood-prone areas require additional insurance and risk premium
rules.message: Area has elevated flood risk — factor in additional insurance costs (~0.3‑0.5% of property value annually).

rules.description: Bushfire-prone areas have stricter building codes
rules.message: Designated bushfire-prone area — new builds must meet BAL (Bushfire Attack Level) requirements, adding ~$20k‑$50k to construction costs.
Vacancy rate above 5% — suggest HOLD or PASS. Rental income may be unreliable; factor in 2‑3 month vacancy periods.

rules.description: Gross yield below 2% is negative cashflow for single dwellings
rules.message: Gross yield below 2% — significant negative cashflow expected. Only suitable for pure capital growth strategy with strong equity buffer.
NSW applies an 8% foreign buyer surcharge on residential property. Overseas investors should factor this into their cost base.

rules.description: VIC has higher land tax for investment properties
rules.message: VIC land tax scales progressively — investment properties above $300k land value incur annual tax. Check State Revenue Office calculator.

rules.description: Suburbs with declining or stagnant population are higher risk
rules.message: Population growth below 0.5% CAGR — limited demand pipeline. Long-term capital growth prospects are weak.
